data.q: What is the main purpose of an interface in PHP?
o: To define a contract for classes
data.e: Interfaces define a contract that implementing classes must follow by providing method definitions.

data.q: Which keyword is used to declare an interface in PHP?
data.e: The interface keyword is used to define an interface in PHP.

data.q: How does a class use an interface?
data.e: A class uses the implements keyword to follow an interface contract.

data.q: Which statement about PHP interfaces is correct?
o: Interfaces can have properties with values
Interfaces cannot be used in PHP
data.e: Interfaces in PHP define only method signatures, not implementations.

data.q: What is a trait in PHP?
data.e: Traits allow code reuse across multiple classes without using inheritance.

data.q: Which keyword is used to define a trait in PHP?
data.e: The trait keyword is used to declare reusable method groups.

data.q: How do you include a trait inside a class?
data.e: Traits are included inside classes using the use keyword.

data.q: Which problem do traits primarily solve?
data.e: Traits help achieve code reuse where multiple inheritance is not supported in PHP.

data.q: Can a PHP class use multiple traits?
o: No, only one trait is allowed
Yes, multiple traits can be used
Only if interfaces are not used
data.e: PHP allows a class to use multiple traits simultaneously.

data.q: What happens if two traits define the same method?
o: It causes a fatal error unless resolved explicitly
The class is converted to abstract
data.e: Method conflicts between traits must be resolved explicitly using conflict resolution syntax.
